response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and real low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his professional C++ exposure is roughly seven months at National Instruments a decade ago, with no Rust at all. His recent years are TypeScript/Node product work rather than systems-level pipeline engineering, and he has no routing, tiling, or navigation-guidance experience. Worth a conversation only if the team is open to a systems-capable polyglot ramping up on C++.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial experience: founded and operates an open-source geo-data mapping platform, deployable on Kubernetes
Low-latency, high-throughput systems background in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Broad full-stack range with strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and Linux/Docker fluency
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015); no Rust experience listed
No experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems specifically
Recent roles (2020-present) are TypeScript/web product work, a drift away from systems and pipeline engineering
